description: Law empowering citizens to access information from public authorities to promote transparency and accountability.

sections.title: Right to Information
sections.text: Subject to the provisions of this Act, all citizens shall have the right to information.
sections.simplified: Every Indian citizen has the right to request information from any government body or public authority. This is a fundamental right.

sections.title: How to File RTI Application
sections.text: A person who desires to obtain any information shall make a request in writing or through electronic means to the Central/State Public Information Officer of the concerned public authority, accompanied by prescribed fee of Rs. 10.
sections.simplified: To file RTI: (1) Write an application addressed to the Public Information Officer (PIO), (2) Pay Rs. 10 fee (BPL cardholders are exempt), (3) Submit by post, email, or in person, (4) You don't need to give reasons for seeking information.

sections.title: Time Limit for Response
sections.text: The PIO shall provide the information within 30 days of receipt of the request. If the information concerns the life or liberty of a person, it shall be provided within 48 hours.
sections.simplified: The government must respond to your RTI within 30 days. If it's about someone's life or liberty, the response must come within 48 hours. If they don't respond, you can file a first appeal.

sections.title: First Appeal and Second Appeal
sections.text: Any person who does not receive a decision within the specified time or is aggrieved by a decision of the PIO may file an appeal within 30 days to the First Appellate Authority. A second appeal lies to the Central/State Information Commission within 90 days.
sections.simplified: If the PIO doesn't respond or you're not satisfied: (1) File First Appeal with the senior officer within 30 days, (2) If still unsatisfied, file Second Appeal with the Information Commission within 90 days.

sections.title: Exemptions from Disclosure
sections.text: Information which may affect sovereignty, security, scientific, economic interests, or cause breach of privilege of Parliament/Legislature, information received in confidence from foreign government, information relating to personal privacy, and cabinet papers are exempt from disclosure.
sections.simplified: Certain information is exempt from RTI: national security matters, cabinet papers, personal privacy of individuals (unless public interest outweighs), trade secrets, and information received from foreign governments.

sections.title: Penalties for PIO
sections.text: Where the CIC/SIC is of opinion that the PIO has refused to receive an application or not furnished information within the time specified or denied request without reasonable cause, it shall impose a penalty of Rs. 250 per day up to a maximum of Rs. 25,000.
sections.simplified: If a Public Information Officer refuses your RTI or doesn't respond within 30 days without valid reason, the Information Commission can fine them Rs. 250 per day (max Rs. 25,000). This ensures accountability.
sections.punishment: Penalty of Rs. 250 per day, max Rs. 25,000 on the PIO
